Well then without any further ado let's bring up the man the legend Dan Kennedy It's just great! Ball to Fire! Music Okay, let's see. I'm going to show you the five best types of copywriting elements for our industry. But I will clean up two things quickly. As I expected last night when we finished I had a couple gentle souls come who were disturbed about where you place the ethical line in all of this. So just for the record my client actually does have five elegantly simple ways to double production in a practice without doing all that ugly stuff you don't want to do. Now as a copywriter I had to help him realize he had five elegantly simple things that could be done in a practice because I wrote the copy first and then we figured out what the five elegantly simple things were. But he actually does have them. You could actually lose a lot of weight on a pizza and ice cream diet if you chose to. First of all there happens to be low carb, low fat, gluten free pizza. Tastes like cardboard but if there's enough stuff on it it's perfectly okay. I consume it. There is soy ice cream and if that's all you ate you might notice by the way that both of those items are in the NutriSystems program. Which if you watch the commercial the implication is that you eat all that stuff in the same day. That's not actually the case. But if you got up in the morning and you had a low carb, gluten free, breakfast pizza and at lunch you had a protein shake and a tiny little cookie. You had dinner, you had another pizza and a salad and then you had a little bowl of low fat, low sugar added, nothing ice cream and that's all you ate compared to what most people eat. You could take weight off of them. The line is everybody draws their own line by the way. I will tell you what a mind and what clients are. And that is if they follow instructions from the deliverable can they get the promised benefits. Now we know most won't follow the instructions. That's not my problem. The only question is if they follow instructions can they get the promised benefits. So the job is to construct the most compelling case you can construct. Which requires you to use the seven strategies we talked about last night in order to then sell a deliverable that will actually produce the core promises if the instructions are followed. So for example I showed you this. I showed you the money hidden in plain sight story. Well there is money hidden in plain sight. It's in every house on every street that people drive past in America. And so it is all about the real estate business and very few people understand that there is money there that they can get right now and that they can get it with no money, no credit, no brains. They don't have to take title. They don't understand these things. So there is money hidden in plain sight. Now the implication people would draw from this is different than the reality. When you say to people there is hidden in plain sight money they like think there is a bag of money behind a tree. That's what's in their head. But the reality is in fact real. And so it qualifies by my line. The other thing I just need to clean up is about the June events. So somebody ran up and told me they were the first to register and that's not true just so you know. There are a lot of advance registrants because people who really pay attention to the newsletters there's been a little invitations to request information and register in advance. So we were at like 72 or 73 when I walked in here last night and there is a hard tap of 183. Any of you who have been to any of my events at that hotel in Cleveland know there really is a hard cap of 183. At 184 David is sitting on Ron's lap for half of the event and Ron is sitting on David's lap for half of the event and it's not a pretty sight. So we stick to the 183. So kind of the race is on. So not only has a see how much of what we talked about last night you can find me doing in those two pieces. But if either of those events does intrigue you you probably want to take care of a registration while you're here. Now number one envy and greener pastures. So there is an old dry hand all the way over here. There is an old Earl Nightingale greener past your story which in case you haven't heard it I will tell it in its shortest least interesting form possible just so you get it. The story is about the farmer who every morning his practices to get his cup of coffee and walk out to the highest hill on his farm and stand on it and look around. And every morning he sees far in the distance his neighbor's farm and its pastures are far greener than are his at his neighbor's farm his neighbor has the same morning habit. He gets up in the morning gets his cup of coffee walks all the way out to the highest hill in his farm stands atop of it and surveys the land around him and is frustrated and disappointed every single day by noticing that in the distance his neighbor's farm land is so much greener than is his. One day they meet each other in town at the feed mill and go across the street to the bar to have a couple of drinks together and there they make a deal somewhat like. Bill gow baited me into this morning. And so they they swap titles. And farmer a goes home to farmer B's farm and farmer B goes home to farmer A's farm and the next morning they each get up get their cup of coffee and walk out to the highest hill in their farms and are shocked and disappointed to notice that their neighbor's farm land is so much greener than is theirs. This is how everybody's mind works. When I used to run mixed dog mastermind groups. Meaning one person's an optometrist the other person owns a shoe store everybody in a group is in a different business. One of the biggest things you had to do is a cop in that kind of group was keep everybody from wanting to swap businesses. Because everybody's thing looks and sounds better at a distance than their own. Which is why it is easier to sell a greener pastures story than it is and improve what you've got story. So in opportunity concepts we talked about the fact that almost everybody in our industry tends to sell one of three things tends to sell rescue repair or improvement. That's really what they tend to sell. If you look at almost all copy in our industry you will find that it is either rescue repair or improvement. But that's all focused on the thing I'm already in. That's all focused on the thing I've already got. It's not focused on an entirely different opportunity an entirely different greener pasture. And it is always easier to sell greener pastures. That doesn't mean it can't be about rescuing repairing or improving the thing they've already got. It just can't be presented as that. So you will often see in my swipe files because I use it a lot some sort of replace your business story. Chiropractic practice, dental practice, restaurant doesn't make any difference. Here's what we're going to do. We're going to replace your business with a better business. It's the same business but we use replacement language in order to present it to people. So greener pastures is a, if you remember the story and you think about your people as the farmers, everybody is always more attracted to that which they see in a distance as desirable than what it really is. So people look at other people's lives and think, oh boy, I wish I was that. And often if in fact they do get that, they discovered that it's different but it isn't necessarily any better than where they were. They trade spouses and discover, huh, different, maybe. But not so great. They switch businesses. They discover, well, look at this. This thing's got its own little set of ugliest and evils. But it is very easy to trigger that in people. Much easier than to trigger the desire to improve or the desire to repair. The desire to replace with something that at a distance looks much better is a much more powerful thing. The link to this is the idea of insider knowledge, insider opportunity, perk and privilege of being able to get something, do something that a lot of other people can't. So I'll show you an old one first. So this is 1921. This is an ad for international correspondent schools. And ICS, you see them advertising today. The correspondent school business is alive and well. ICS is alive and well. They were a client of mine in the 1980s, 60 years after this ad. They'll be around, perhaps digital eventually, but they'll be around forever. So this is a picture of people, husband now with wife and child at new home. The proudest moment of our lives have come. The way to get that is with a new career, which you pick from this list of new careers. This story is much more believable attached to new careers than if we attached it to something that helped the guy with the hat do better in the career he was already in. Because that's harder for him to believe. This is a more contemporary. So we'll just, this is now about place. So you look out your window past your gardener who is busily pruning the lemon cherry and fig trees amidst the splendor of gardenia's pibiscous and hollyhocks. The sky is clear blue. The sea is a deeper blue sparkling with sunlight. A gentle breeze comes drifting in from the ocean, clean and refreshing as your maid brings you breakfast in bed. For a moment you think you have died and gone to heaven, but this paradise is real and affordable. In fact, it costs only half as much to live in this dream lifestyle as a wood to stay in your own home. This is a 16 page sales letter for the International Living Newsletter, which tells you how to move to some God forsaken, bug infested, dangerous place in which you need armed guards around the perimeter. However, the good news is you can buy the whole thing for $5.85 a month. When I lived in Phoenix, I was road water. I traveled a lot and I flew commercial. So often you're flying home to Phoenix and the person next to you, the question usually asks just about the time that you can see if a night, all the lights or day you can see. The question asks are you coming here on business or vacation or do you live here? I live here. Oh, you live here. Oh, I wish I lived here. And then they tell you all the horrible crap about Wichita. You know, I live in Wichita and oh, four months out of the year, you just freeze to death. There's four months out of the year in Phoenix. You cook, but it's okay. There's all this snow and the people are all fat and ugly. So they do 10 minutes about what this, what, how horrible it is, you know, to live in Wichita and how they all, how they wish they lived in Phoenix. 60% of the people who moved to Phoenix leave after the first July. Okay, there's a big, there's a big churn rate, by the way. And of course, I would always say to them, well, Wichita has these big green signs. What arrows on them that tell you how to get the hell out of Wichita. You can leave anytime you want. The federal government has not yet done population apportionment and rationing and all that to make you stay in Wichita. They'll get around to it, but right now you can leave, you know, but people, they don't get that. But they have in their head, you know, that this place is paradise. Not so, and certainly Costa Rica ain't. But the guy in Wichita, this sounds really, really, really good to him. The elitist thing, this is an old sales letter of mine. Many of you will have seen it. So welcome to a radical underground. Who else wants to defy all normal, common, ordinary, customary boundaries and restrictions on making money on the speedies, the independence, well, hell, who doesn't? All right, which is the point. It's exactly the point. There is a mandate in all this to be different. Here is a current control from Klein and mine health source Chris Tomshack. They franchise chiropractic clinics. This is a current control postcard. If you are finally at the point when you are thinking, enough with the seminars, the coaching programs, the consultants, the prepackaged single condition marketing programs. For neuropathy, fibromyalgia, et cetera. Enough, enough, enough already. There must be a better way. There must be a better way. And here you go immediately here, watch the video and download the free report or call and listen to a brief recorded message and we'll send the stuff to you. And as you can see, there's a big, you know, no sign over a picture of a seminar room with trust me, I'm a guru written on the white screen. All right, this will invite them to a seminar held by a guru who will sell them a franchise. The irony aside, there's a mandate to be different. So what Greener Pastures is all about is positioning what you do has different from everything else being sold to your target market. This requires some creative thought. It requires some disregard of irony. It regards some ability to make the switch when they finally see what it is that you are presenting. It's really not another seminar. It's a discovery day event. And it's not coaching. It's coaching packaged as a franchise. So there is reality to the claim, but there is also poetic license to the claim. But so the first thing you want to get is the mandate to be different. As we've gone along, I've also given you some words. I gave you some last night. Another great word that's the client of mine, Chris Cardell, some of you are here because of Chris, I think. So this is a really good word. Finally, it suggests that everything that they have seen up until now is one thing. And finally, there is a different thing. It's a very good word. This is, finally, all of your marketing is done for you. For you, words I gave you last night. Announcing the complete digital marketing system. The instant marketing system to plug into your business to bring you, understand the mental picture they get? Plug. I can manage that. I plug in crap all the time. Do that. The instant marketing system to plug into your business to bring you an endless stream of new customers, all of your essential marketing done for you in just 30 days. Now that meets our mandate to be different. If you insist on presenting what it is that you sell to your market has just better, you are at extreme disadvantage. They have heard better a lot. There is a mandate to present it has different. Second item. Urgency. We leave large numbers of people and enormous sums of money at the point of being almost persuaded. So in the chain of events that have to happen for there to be an exchange of value for money, the last one is they have to hand over the money. If you get them almost to the point of doing that, but then you allow time for them to consider it, most of them will go away. That is why studying the old stuff is so cool because the old stuff had built in an invitation to procrastinate. Because almost every response from a distance had built in delay. Somebody heard a radio commercial and had to wait until they could get to a landline phone to call. So they had an opportunity to decide not to call. To forget not to call. Somebody is going to order from that international correspondent school ad, they have to fill out the coupon, clip out the coupon, they have to go find an envelope, they have to put it in an envelope, they have to put a stamp on it, they have to put it in the mail. Built in delay. We don't have that in our world today. You have all sorts of ways to eliminate the forced delay. But you have huge numbers of people still who almost get to the phone. So spend time on a website and then bail out on the order form, etc., etc. And largely if they do, they are gone. In effect, you have to start all over. You really have to start all over. So urgency is very important. There needs to be a sense of now or never. So here is your urgency list. Here are the things, the copy elements most used to create urgency. There is some version of the one that got away story. And pretty much everybody knows one of these in their lives. There is usually a passed down family story about grandpa who Ray Kroc offered him all the franchise rights to McDonald's for a dollar and ten cents in Tupelo and he turned him down. Almost every family has some version of that story. If you are a Disney in Orlando and you drive out the kind of back end into Kissimmee, there is now a customer gate there. There is a three story McDonald's on the left side as you are driving out. It has driving ramps up to all three stories. It is huge, it is glass, it is this mega mega mega mega McDonald's on this piece of land. That used to be just an employee entrance and exit back there. There was nothing going on in Kissimmee and that was an empty piece of land that nobody really placed an enormous amount of value on. None of the whole place. So Roddy Tolleson and I had an option on that piece of land which cost us 50 bucks a month to maintain the option. And a month came when neither one of us had 50 bucks. And every time I drive past the three story McDonald's on the piece of land at the backside of Disney in Kissimmee, I hurt. Even thinking about it now. I like a lot of you but I probably wouldn't be here if I just had 50 bucks. So everybody has a sense of one that got away. So you can stimulate it. You can create the emotional pain attached to it back to one of our seven items from last night. There are fears of course. We talked about the fear list last night. So urgency is created by a fear of being left behind, left out, being uncool. Why does everybody go stand in line to get the new smartphone? They don't want to be uncool. They don't want to be left behind. A lot of people think it's because they want to be first. No. No. They don't want to be last. They don't want to be late to the party. Fear of regret. Easier to stimulate older customers than younger customers. By the way, boomer senior population, great trigger. Scarcity, simply it's good to go away. And there's not going to be any more. In the Hemmaker-Schlemer mail order catalog, Christmas catalog this year, they're selling a $200,000 Batmobile. The brand new one. The one that was in the TV show like we had with Adam West here. But a brand new one. Yeah, it's only 200K. What a lot of people don't know is they make some of those every single year. And they make new ones and they sell them. Most people would assume there's only one. So there's an urgency to get it before you can't get it. It's not stated. It's just in this case an assumption most people would make. I happen to know they make a dozen every year. So if somebody got there first, they would still take your 200K for the next one. But scarcity, exclusivity. So that's like the next best thing to scarcity. The scarcity is it's all going to go away and there won't be any more period. Exclusivity is there's a limited amount of it and only a certain number of people will be able to get it and then it will stop. That, by the way, is a very good trigger with relatively affluent customers. Successful people. Because after all, what is the point of being rich except to have things other people can't have? That's the whole point. And so that trigger works pretty well. Here's some actual copy. Here's a fear of regret template. If you went through all of my work for 40 years, you would find this reused six bazillion times in client copy. It's not every day that a millionaire blank fill in the blank. A millionaire plumber knocks on your door and offers to blank. It's not every day that a millionaire real estate investor, it's not every day that a, you know, whatever fill in the blank, arrives, knocks on your door and offers to take you under their wing and reveal their secret formula to you. That's a template and it triggers fear of regret because it's not every day. So if I pass this up, what will happen? How will I feel 20 years from now if I did not take advantage of that offer? Here's one that got away copy. It's fairly recent. I wrote this for a client just a few weeks ago. An early partner of Jeff Bezos exited Amazon with a payment of $500. Tom Monahan's original partner in Domino's quit and took a used Volkswagen. Vision isn't easy. As a result, many people let the opportunity of lifetime slip through their fingers and live to regret it. But in this case, I think I can make it easy for you not to let this one slip through your fingers. Then goes into risk reversal guarantee copy, of course, so that you can take advantage of this opportunity of lifetime with zero risk. So that's one that got away copy. I'll show you another piece. This is very current. So here's some urgency copy. This is toward the end of a long sales letter. I'll tell you again by telling you why I think your future hangs in the balance, why this is so vitally important now. We live in very uncertain times. I think back to the Lehman Brothers Weekend of Death and the crash of the entire U.S. economy that followed. Well, it's a slight exaggeration, but maybe the crash in your 401k and IRA too. Down here, this is no time to be skating on such thin ice. This is no time to be playing around with untested ideas and untested leaders. This is the time to strengthen your practice. Continues. If you, in honest time with yourself, feel that there are weaknesses and vulnerabilities in your practice. If you feel anxious about having enough new patients, if you worry that your practice won't be valuable enough, they might not have been worrying about that stuff until I just said it. Now they are worrying about that stuff. And here's your urgency copy. Please act decisively. The urgency is real. The deadline is real. Discounts and bonuses end with it. The likelihood the summit sells out early is also real. The benefits of being here are proun- the timing is vital. So that's urgency copy that creates anxiety. So loop back to your seven from last night. It's urgency copy that creates anxiety, uncertainty, angst. And when people are anxious, what do they want to do? Be unanxious. So an act can occur that relieves the anxiety. What's the act? Sign up. Purchase. The purchase is the solution. Now in reality, of course, if the guy doesn't come to the seminar and take home the big list of stuff to do, and he doesn't, because the five elegantly simple things have 50 sub things, if he doesn't go home and use this, he isn't really, but he feels at that moment, all that anxiety you created, he feels it all go away because he has filled out the registration form and popped it into his fax machine. This is the game we are in. Third, here's a fascinating category of copy. It is called contradiction copy. Contradiction copy says, which were you good to believe? Your lying eyes are me. Contradiction copy says, no, the sky is red. So for example, people want security and safety. They believe there's safety in numbers. It's a foolish belief, but people believe it. People in the room of my age, when parents like exercise some control over your dating, foreign concept, now I realize, but people of my age, when parents exercised, mothers got their daughters to double date because there was safety in numbers. Actually, it just provided people to take turns as a lookout. But they believe there was safety in numbers. However, the problem with safety in numbers is it's not new and exciting because if there's six bazillion people doing it, so we have to with copy be able to say that there's safety in numbers, but you are still getting in on a ground floor and getting secrets nobody knows about. We have to be able to pull that off. People want things that are tested and proven. Nobody likes to be a lab rat or a guinea pig. However, people also want a new breakthrough. We have to be able to put those two things together in the same sentence and make them work. Secret, tens of thousands, however, are in on it. Wall Street is evil. However, when you invest in stocks with my advice, you win. Everybody there is a crook. It's a giant casino. All they do is steal your money. Now come with me and let's go play in the casino. Bad timing is good timing. Almost everybody, by the way, thinks the timing to do whatever it is you want them to do right at the moment is bad. It's either generally bad, the economy sucks, our industry is in trouble, or it's specifically bad. You know, I got four young kids at home. I should wait until they all, you know. It's a great joke about the 90-year-old couple in front of the judge and divorce court. Why on earth have you waited? They're 55 years. Why are they waiting for the kids to die? So here's some, it's a little dark, I guess. So here's some contradiction copy. I will read. I have personally done more than 1200 transactions. I continue to actively buy, sell, rehab, flip, find for others, and invest right now as my business. That's important. I'm not just getting a few deals made to claim I'm doing what I teach. This is my business, subhead not, as seen on TV. I'm nothing like the get rich in real estate hot shots you see on late night and weekend TV, because I haven't yet figured out how to get on late night. I have participated in nearly $200 million of real estate transactions. I've done a lot of small, nicely profitable deals. My biggest was acquiring 9.8 million of rental properties at half price. I did nothing but deals from 2003 to 2008 when I started coaching other investors. Then I built the most completely automated business system this industry has ever seen. So you can do all your business on your smartphone. Then I built the nation's largest unique network of active investors and buyers so that there's someone waiting for every good deal any student of mine finds. This is important. Only after perfecting my methods from 2003 to 2008 did I then start sharing my knowledge with others. Let's stop there for a second. Everything we've just said is old. It's tested. It's proven. It dates back to 2003. Everything I've just said up until now has cobwebs on it. Now, with all that as foundation, I have built a radically new system. The producers, profits at any market good or bad in ways no one in the real estate industry has ever imagined possible. This is so different from anything this field has ever seen. There are plenty of skeptics and deniers. There are even haters because they work so hard and put up with so much suffering for their money. Well, I and my students are making money so easily. What they think, heck, what they don't want you to know doesn't matter. This kind of copywriting, if you really examine it again and again and again and again, it's a lot. It's the same ingredients being put in the pot and put together every single time. What did I wind up with? I wound up with secrets they don't want you to know. Never before has this business been made so plug and play. Plug, people get that. I can plug in something. I've managed to do that three or four times without electrocuting myself. You can simply find qualified properties that can be bought in certain ways, post them to our members only online media, like using match.com to attract dates or monster.com to attract job interviews. That's a copy technique not on my list, but you want to remember it. It's called taste like chicken. Well, you know the answer to everything, every horrible thing anybody wants to get you to eat, rattlesnake, roadkill, fried warthog, whatever, your question is, well, what does it taste like? And their answer is always it tastes like chicken, which my answer is, well, then let's eat chicken. However, the taste like chicken copy technique is to take something that people don't understand and hook it to something that they do understand. So I didn't have it on my list. It's a minor matter, not one of the major five, but it's still a good technique to know. So like using match.com to attract dates or monster.com to attract job interviews, have buyers come forward, make the match, make money, never actually own the properties, let alone play landlord, borrow from banks or otherwise do difficult, risky or time consuming work. What's that paragraph from last night? Somebody? Well, no, no, no. Yeah, yeah, yeah, exactly. Thank you. That paragraph is you can get everything you want and do nothing you don't like. That's what that paragraph is, right? I'm releasing my new system to a very limited number. What did I do? I made it old, tested, proven, reliable as a rambler, and then I made it radically new and secret and exclusive. Contradiction copy. I'll call it paper on my shoe. Equivalent. Post it now. So here's the, here's a control piece. It's been a control piece now for five years for a client of mine. It may well be one of the best pieces I've ever written in terms of results. Certainly not in terms of creative genius because it is, it is the kitchen sink piece of all time. It is taking every single thing we talked about last night and every single thing we talked about this morning and shoving it into the same four pages. It is also all pieces of previously used copy from somewhere else stuck together in the same piece. This is me with three by five cards and glue. This is what this is. So it ain't elegant and it is full of contradiction copy about the economy. This is my let President Obama make you rich copy. Which the client has never let me do, let President Obama make you rich and you'll be the only one. He thought that was counterproductive. So this is a pearls piece. It drives you to a website with your name in it. Crazy Texas millionaire. So Crazy Texas millionaire I have used off and on for these clients for 22 years. Took it out of the stack, stuck it in here. So inside where do we pick that back up? Here we go. My name is Phil Grove. I'm a nationally known business man author and coach to experience and brand new business people. Maybe you've heard of me. By the way, that's a great line. You want to write that one down. Maybe you've heard of me. They haven't but it suggests everybody else has and they like are just out of it. My friends call me the Crazy Texas Billionaire but believe me I'm crazy like a fox and I have turned the lousy economy into an amazing new opportunity. So Crazy Texas Billionaire has been the whole ad in the past but here it is stuck in this. Then I have let President Obama make you rich. Free celebrity. Alright. Here's the contradiction copy. This bad economy is sticking around and it may get worse but it could be your best friend. Because of the lousy economy there's a ton of money that could be going into your pocket right now easier than ever and I will help make it happen. By the way, what do all people who aren't getting the financial results they want in life believe? It's a bad economy. Alright. Not I'm screwed up. No, no, no. It's a bad economy. So okay. This is a given. Right. Let's see if I can find some other contradiction copy for you. Crazy Texas Billionaire spills the beans on how insiders are profiting big time from the real estate crash and lousy economy. Understand here we are selling real estate investing and I have just used the words real estate crash. This is like inviting you to my restaurant with words like toxic gas and poison. Contradiction copy. It's a good say. Oh I have just other pieces so I have unusual money making hobby. That's a piece from somewhere else. Right. Hobby, it's a good word to write that one down too. See because it sounds like not much work. Okay. See you don't want to say unusual money making work. That's a bad idea. Hobby has a nice connotation. Trying to see if I could. No that's about it. That categorizes his contradiction. Next. Unconditional love copy. This is pretty essential. First of all, it absolutely cannot be their fault. The most unwelcome message in the world is your unsatisfactory condition is caused by you. This is a unpleasant, unappealing, pretty much unsalable proposition. It's largely true but it is an unsalable proposition. So not only don't you ever want to make it, you want to be as far at the opposite end of that continuum as you possibly can be. So it can't be their fault and you want to tell them it's not their fault. You can loop it back to last night under mysticism to conspiracy if you wish. That's one way to get there. But you just cannot tell a poor person they're poor because they choose to be poor. You cannot tell a fat person they're fat because they choose to be fat. It's an unacceptable thing to do. It's been tested by the way a lot. Halbert tried a campaign telling fat people that they're fat because they were fat. He did it as well as any copywriter could possibly write it but it bombed because nobody wants to hear that. They wish to hear this opposite. My unsatisfactory situation, whatever that is, their relationship, their health, their physical fitness, their finances, their annoying customers, their annoying employees, whatever their unsatisfactory condition is, you win a lot of points when you hug them and tell them it's not your fault. That's what their mama did which has caused a lot of their problems. She hugged them and said it's not your fault. All the other children are evil and mean. It's not your fault. People just don't understand you. The guy is moving back home for the fifth time after his fifth divorce and mama is telling him it's not your fault. All those women are evil. At one it's a 50-50 proposition. At two, at five, the common denominator in this equation of misery is not the five spouses. It's you, bubba. It's mathematically impossible to pick five evil bitches in a row. You can't do it. So when we hug them with our copy and we tell them it is not their fault for whatever now reason we have, there are secrets who have been withheld from them, there is a conspiracy against them. Every way they have been shown so far to do X is so impossibly difficult and ridiculous that only a few people could possibly do it. For whatever reason when we hug them with our copy and tell them it is not their fault, we gain a lot of points. Like all the points we ever need. If you try telling them the other thing, you lose all your points. So the copy hug is it's not your fault that you are in this unsatisfactory situation because now fill in the because blank. That's your copy template. And it needs to feel kind of like a little hug, a little pat, and a little reassurance to whatever degree you can say it. Next, they have to be told their past doesn't matter. So one of the great lines in personal power one, the first Guthrie-Renker infomercial with Tony, one of the great lines written in that show is, your past need not control your future. What a great line. I can't take credit for it. I think it's Lenny Lieberman's, but nonetheless, it is a summation of what people must believe to have optimism about their future because the reason they're paying attention to you in the first place is because their past is full of unsatisfactory outcomes. Here's who will not be at your free preview about how to get rich in real estate. Trump. He ain't coming. He's got a fairly satisfactory outcome in the money department. People who are there have unsatisfactory outcomes in their past. And the older they are, the more of them have accumulated. The knapsack just gets bigger and bigger and bigger. And it has more weight the older they get because they can see the end. So we have to tell them don't worry about any of that. Don't worry about the garage full of soap and vitamins you have. Don't worry about any of that. Oh, Xcon, no problem. Your past doesn't matter. Next, I understand you. They want to engage in dialogue with somebody who gets them. And that really gets them because if you really get them, you know, all their unsatisfactory outcomes are all their fault. They're probably incompetent. They're going to be a major project to help. They don't want you to understand them at that level. They want you to understand how they feel and the difficulty of their life's struggle. Because everybody thinks they are engaged in an epic struggle. Most people have no idea, of course. First of all, if you were born like here or someplace like here, we can make a list of countries that if by accident of birth you were born there, you would flat out no struggle. But everybody thinks they're engaged in an epic struggle. And so they want to be understood. One of the ways you know you got this right is anecdotally, when people say to you, and in the key markets in which I write copy for, I get it a lot, it sounds like you were under the kitchen table at two o'clock in the morning when I and my wife were having our conversations. Exactly. Now you have to know what that conversation sounds like in order to get that, which is why it's harder to write copy to groups you have no affinity for than it is to write copy for groups you do have affinity for. If you are going to write copy, by the way, for people you have no affinity for, you need to let go hang out. So if you're going to write copy for engineers who have corporate jobs and you aren't one of them, you need to go find the bar down the street from the Motorola factory and you need to for the next two weeks hang out, dress yourself up like an engineer. Kind of dull look on your face, gray skin, pocket protector. The most complicated smartphone known to man with six antennas, shoes with the little holes in the toes and laces and go in there and hang out from about four o'clock on. I mean you got to do it because you got to hit this. I think this is a story that I was telling you about. The first time I heard it, I thought it was on the list of stupid shit you hear from people. So this goes back to Gail's probably the only person who Rubell know the person. So this goes back to Kevin Roberts. So Kevin Roberts line was they don't care how much you know until they know how much you care. The first time I heard it, I'm like, hey, hey, hey, hey, hey, hey, hey, hey, hey, hey. I had that reaction to five or six things Kevin said and by the way it turns out they are all wise. And this is one of them. If they don't feel like you get them and you really care about them, then you have an uphill battle. I appreciate your struggle. If you read carefully, you will see these two pieces personally in every book and many issues of our newsletter. You will see me do it again and again and again and again. And I do understand the small business owner and the entrepreneur. And I do appreciate your struggle every day. I get it. I live with exactly the same conspiracy against productivity and accomplish but that you do. Every morning we leave the house and people start throwing barriers and hurdles and roadblocks and orange cones and everything in front of us. I get it. And by the end of the day, even though you didn't lift a heavy object, you are like whipped. I get that. And entrepreneurs want to know, I get it. So I keep telling you so you know I get it. You deserve to be whatever the outcome here is, the satisfactory exceptional outcome is you are promising. You deserve to be rich. You deserve a break today. You deserve to be loved. You deserve to be thin. You deserve... People are hardwired to get entitlement as love. That's why once we started the entitlement society, it is so hard to put that Pandora back in the box. Because every time somebody steps forward politically and creates a new entitlement, it is perceived as love and caring and affection. It gives people a warm feeling that these people in Washington or in the state capital care so much about them that they are going to create and give this to them. You remember Bill Clinton's I Feel Your Pain. So you deserve are another two very powerful words. Put them on your word list. So the founders gave us what? Opportunity and the right to pursuit of. What if people translated it to? Supposed to have. A home is a right. Not the opportunity to maybe get one. No, no, no, home is a right. Health care is a right. The extension of course, well you have a car, I don't have a car. We got to cut your car in half so we each have half a car or you got to share your car with me or you got to go buy me a car because I deserve a car. Why do you deserve a car and I don't deserve a car? I deserve a car. Then the next extension is you got a better car than I got. Well that's not fair. I deserve a nice new Lexus just like you got a nice new Lexus. Why should I drive an old Chevy? The fact that I don't have a job and I don't work and I don't, it's irrelevant. So this is in there. It's easy to stimulate and you want to stimulate it so you deserve. So let me show you a piece of copy that falls into this category as well as some others. This is also very current copy, very current. It's for a Pearl's postcard so it says insert name at the top. If your spouse is threatening to divorce you, if you go to one more seminar or buy one more course, by the way I just hit about 75% of the people were mailing. See I know the conversation, I know that conversation. No you are not going to another seminar Charlie. If your spouse is threatening to divorce you, if you go to one more seminar or buy one more course, by the way I have a few words with you. Enough learning, how about some doing? One weekend real estate seminar just makes you dangerous to yourself and others. But trying to learn everything before making money puts you on a treadmill to know where forever getting ready, never getting going. Stop making money now. Forgive me I'll just read the rest of it. Why do so many with honest ambition never get started, stall or fail at real estate investing? I'm about to make it not their fault. The hug is coming. Why do so many with honest ambition never get started, stall or fail at real estate investing? They are the real reasons. They don't follow the instructions. They go to the seminar, they buy some crap from Ron or whoever and I say crap like I say glop. I don't mean it like manure, I mean it like our stuff. So they buy some stuff and they take it home and they put it under the bed and they sleep above it. And then they check their bank account in a month and see how many real estate transactions they've done. That's why. Or they go out and the very first person who says no to them, that's it. They can only take one note but we're not going to say that. There's no profit in saying that. We're going to give them a hug. Why do so many with honest ambition never get started, stall or fail at real estate investing? It is after all the cornerstone of all wealth. You know people succeed at flipping houses. There are even reality TV shows about it. By the way, that's the same as maybe you've heard of me. You know, well I didn't know but since you said I know, I guess I know. You know people who've gone rich buying, holding and renting properties. Yet I will tell you my own study of this shows fewer than 15% of the people who try succeed. With that in mind, I decided to do something to let earnest people apprentice and earn while they learn and be guaranteed that they actually make money, not just spend it on seminars and courses. Who am I? And then there's some credential copy. And so if you are tired of learning, preparing, waiting, maybe procrastinating and ready to really get ahead financially, I'm ready to take you under my wing. Here are the four reasons people don't get anywhere at this and how I have fixed them. And all four reasons have nothing to do with personal initiative, responsibility, discipline, application of information. They have nothing to do with any of that. They have to do with flaws in the way everybody has been told to do it up until now. Last category I think. Yeah. Reasons why I've arrived. Copy. People think, so if we told the truth, here would be our reason why. To get your money. With value in exchange. But to get your money. So if there's 15,000 people in an arena, and I'm the last speaker of the day, and I walk out and the first thing I say is, just so we're clear, the only reason I'm here this afternoon is to get money from as many of you as humanly possible. Exactly how many points do I get for such honesty? No, not zero. That would appeal to a certain number of people. They would be all struck by the candor. But not enough of them to feed very many horses. And I work for oats and vet bills, so I have to keep that in mind. There are 26 children back home who must be fed. So that's not an option. I can't start a sales letter that way. I can't start a webcast that way. I can't do any of that that way. You will hear people, so the big lie at the other end is, oh, I love this so much, I would do it for free. Really? Offer them an opportunity to do it for free. See how many leap. I referred to them earlier, our friend Wayne Dyer, who even can put himself in four places simultaneously, wouldn't take that offer up from any of the four. No. But that kind of candor, people want to believe that there is a different reason for why you do what you do and for why you have arrived in front of them at this moment in time. And hopefully, by the way, there is another reason. Hopefully, you are doing what you're doing and arriving because you genuinely want to deliver benefit. You are interested in what it is that you do. Hopefully, there are other reasons. But the number one reason, if we're business people, has to be a business reason. And by the way, if your number one reason is not a business reason, you would be well advised to be involved in social work, charity work, religious work, whatever, not in business because you're dangerous to yourself. You make all sorts of decisions for bad reasons. So they want to know why you have arrived in front of them. And there are three categories of answers. So one is the logical category. I'm here because you've been an entrepreneur magazine subscriber for five consecutive years. And based on that, I assume X, Y, and Z. That's a logical reason why I've arrived in your mailbox. So if my research about you is right, you were not picked at random, which cannot be followed by how we picked you. We need smarter plumbers. We need real estate finders. We need chiropractors to handle a thousand new patients a month in Summitville, Pennsylvania, right now. This piece that I showed you also has that. This piece is person specific and area specific. So it is dropped in. And just for finding certain homes in the Simpsonville area, you get paid, yada, yada. And somewhere in here it says we desperately need somebody in Simpsonville right now. So those are logical reasons. A second category of reason is mission reason. I'm mad as hell. So I've come out of retirement because all these charlatans are selling you crap and it aggravates me beyond all belief. And here I am now to stand against the charlatans and the fakers and the pretenders and give you the true secrets about commodity investing. Every time I open the investors business daily and see one of those crackpot lying ads, it sets my hackles afire. And so even though I have no need to work, I'd rather not work. My wife doesn't want me to work. I'm here because I can't stand to see good, honest people ripped off by these liars. That's the I'm mad as hell copy. I'm out to prove. Sick and tired of all this malaise and nobody can be self-reliant anymore and opportunity in America is dead and the American dream no longer exists. And I lie awake at night with the frustration of all this. So I am out to prove all the doubters wrong and take 1000 ordinary men and women and make them multi-millionaires in 36 months or less. Do you want to be one of the that's the out to prove. There's the giving something back. I'm here because baseball been very, very, very, very good to me. Most of you don't remember. So I'm here to give something back. There's a headline. I want to give this to you before it's too late. It was a headline of a full page ad in the opportunity world. The ad ran for 30 some odd years every single month and six or seven magazines. It was a great story about a guy who had now had been told he only had six months to live. The ad ran for 33 years. Well, it said he'd been told he only had six months to live. It didn't, you know, and so he didn't want to take his secret to the grave. And so I want to give this to you before it's too late. These are mission reasons. Right. Then we have the mystical reasons. The right audience really likes the mystical reasons. That's some version of when the student is ready, the teacher will appear. Which is actually when the student is ready, the salesman will appear. So somebody has brought us together in this place, in this moment in time, for a mystical reason. Pick the somebody that works for you and or more importantly for your audience. Okay. Could be God, could be the universe, could be the magic frog Frodo. I don't care. Okay, but that's that story. So this is a great line of copy. It's an occurrence sales letter of mine. Trust what your inner mind tells you. Which is what we just told you your inner mind is saying to you by the way. So there are logical reasons. There are mission reasons. There are mystical reasons. You may pick one from any of the columns you like depending on your audience or you may mix them. You may find a way to put all three of them together. But it is an important copy element. I'll show you a couple of examples. This is also instructor for a different reason. So this is very current copy. In actual deliverable it'll look like the let Obama make you rich piece. But this is a health piece. So it'll be personalized to the person. Martha, it drives them to a pearl site. It's all about pain relief. Let me give you the important lines that are in the category of copy we're talking about. Hi, I'm Dr. John Frank and MD in private practice with important news for you. If you suffer from chronic continuing joint pain, back pain or arthritis pain. I'm also a retired professional football player with a Super Bowl ring from the San Francisco 49ers. From playing on the same team as greats like Joe Montana and Dwight Clark. As an aside that's important because nobody knows who the hell John Frank is. But kind of everybody at this age knows who Joe Montana is. So by renting John Frank I got Joe Montana. I got a picture of Frank with Joe Montana. I got, we can't afford Joe Montana. Here's the key line. As a football player I promise you I know about pain firsthand. I understand you, I appreciate your struggle. That's why I'm here. Up close and personal. There's not a pro ball player alive who doesn't suffer neck, back, knee or other pain. Not just while they play but after they retire as they age. Most of us develop arthritis, blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah. Opening a jar, tying a shoelace, gardening, golfing, hoisting your grand kid into a car seat. I get it and I am here with real help. By the way, those items, opening a jar, tying a shoelace, gardening, golfing, hoisting your grand kid into a car seat. Are the exact items that surveys of arthritis sufferers complain about ranked in the order in which they rank them. A copy is not accidental. Not great copy. You don't just sit down and throw the shit up in the air and whatever order it comes down in, you put it in the copy. Not if you want to win and not if you want to win in tough categories. So I get it. I am here with real help. Next sentence, if you want to steal words, the start of the next sentence is a good one. What I'd like you to have. Those are great words. Not by the way what I'd like to sell you. What I'd like you to buy. No, what I'd like you to have. Now this just as an aside, an interesting piece of trivia about this is so this one, this version of this, the one in my hand sells glop, not information. And so it, toward the end of the front of the postcard, it shifts into, I assure you as a doctor this is very different from any off the shelf products at the drug store. Products you see advertised on TV or in magazines and it is safer than prescription drugs because so this sells glop. This version of it sells information. What I'd like you to have is a copy of the big book of pain free living. This giant book is full of expert advice about things you can do for yourself that really work pain relief miracles. Foods to eat, foods to never eat. There are ways to get a better night's sleep, wake up rested and ready to go at any age. This big book is the most complete, most authoritative, most useful guidebook upon pain relief ever compiled. The rest of it is all same. Just one sells glop, the other one sells book. Just as a marketing lesson, then this split tests and it split tests mixed. So the bonus with the glop is the book. The bonus with the book is your first month of glop free. Then we start whacking your card forever more until you finally die of your ailment. And keep delivering you glop. So that's split tests, split tests, split tests, split tests. Then of course you're splitting against lists. So this is like a 25 different things split test matrix and about 100 grand to actually run the test to know something. You could do it cheaper in a different way, but I just wanted to tell you what was going on. So there's a reason for this guy arriving to tell you this story. It has little elements of, I'm at his hell, it has a little element of mysticism, new, etc. But its main element is I'm a guy who has the same pain you got. I get it. I finally found something that works. Why keep it to myself? That's why I'm here. He also happens to, you know, he's kind of a trifecta because he's an MD. He's an athlete and he's into alternative health. Here's a more logical, standard, ordinary. So this is a control piece for many years from Newsweek. Dear reader, if the list upon which I found your name is any indication, this is not the first nor will it be the last subscription letter you receive. So we have told them, we found them on a list of subscribers. That's why you're getting a subscription pitch. This is from Agora. This is a Oxford Club control. Dear fellow investor, that's a very good word by the way. Add it to your word list. Make yourself the peer. So, dear Success Magazine reader, what's better? Dear fellow Success Magazine reader. Ah, we're both Success Magazine readers. That's nice. Dear fellow investor, you have been chosen from a select list to receive an invitation into what must be the world's most remarkable and profitable financial alliance. You have been chosen from a select list. I guess it okay. They went and found me on a list. Now, you may have some curiosity about what list, but we have appeased his curiosity about and we have given him a reason why we are arriving to talk to him now. So let's wrap up with the last big hurdle or the big overarching hurdle, which is how we get to a sufficient amount of trust for someone to pull the trigger and hand us money. So this is not new information. It's in books, but I want to tie it to this for you. So the first thing to know is that the problem for most people is not just distrust of you. It is not that they don't trust salespeople. It's they don't trust themselves with salespeople. So during the bleak years of real estate, the biggest problem that marketers to agents, not the get rich in real estate to investor guys, but guys like my client Craig Proctor and Kinder and Reese who market to licensed agents. Their biggest problem was all of a sudden nobody would open their email. Why? Two reasons. One, a pre belief that there's nothing that can be done right now to improve anything and B, I don't trust myself to read the email because I might buy something and I don't want to buy anything. Therefore, I won't even tempt myself. I like, you know, trying not to drive near a donut shop. I know I can't trust myself. I don't stop at a convenience store to get a cup of coffee in our area because they're all got Krispy Kreme's in the counter when you walk in by the coffee. Guess who ain't just getting a cup of coffee? I'll only have, you know, I'll jog it off later. Iron tribe guys will find this funny. Jake wanted me to do push ups with them. I said, how about if I sit on you and you do push ups and show everybody how strong you are? How about if we do that? So people don't trust themselves to even read your sales letter. That's where they are. So it's not just about you. It's about them. That's how extreme it is. So there's nine gates to customer commitment that all have to do with finally getting to trust. This list is in the trust based marketing book. So if you got it, you got it, but you want to tie it to this. So here's what people are trying to figure out. Is he for real? If it's in a person voice, if you're writing from you or you're writing for somebody in a person voice, dear, you don't have to take a picture of it. It's in a $14 book. You know, I got horses to feed. Break out, you know, break out 10 bucks by yourself. No, it's okay. Go ahead. I'm just, it's all right. So is he for real? Meaning is he authentic? Does he sound authentic to me? Does he sound real to me? And that's why sometimes a little bit of candor is okay. If you do it, you wrap it properly. The try and, is this a person or is this a made up fabricated character? So some of the best platform sales people and understand that copy, that's copy you see. You don't, the best platform sales people don't get up here and do stuff spontaneously. They've scripted this thing. The best ones work to a great degree because people see them as being authentic. Not perfect, by the way. Is he telling me the truth? So can I believe him? Is there believability to what I'm hearing, what I'm reading? So if you go back to my enough learning how about some doing, get this line. Yet I will tell you my own study of this shows fewer than 15% of the people who try succeed. Now on the surface you would think, why do we want to remind people that 85% of the people who take a whack at this fail? Well because the minute they hear it, they say, well this guy's telling me the truth. When you present the ugly part, you have to be careful how you do it, but when you do it, up goes believability. Is he knowledgeable and competent? People want to know if they're going to give you money, do you actually know what the hell you're doing? Can you actually help them? Are you competent? This is important. Is he appropriate for me? It's called feasibility of relationship, which is why affinity matches in the first place are so important. Different people resonate with different people. As incredible as it may seem to you, there are people who actually are totally turned off by me. This is one of the great mysteries of life, but it exists. And they may really like somebody else. So what are people trying to figure out? They're trying to figure out, is this a feasible relationship? Am I going to like being in a working relationship, in a coaching environment, in a dialogue with this person? Number five, it's very hard to do at a distance. Is he listening or is he just peddling? People want customized solutions, not one size fit. It's the toughest kind of copy to write, but it is there. Six, is he reliable? Is he still going to be around six months from now? Because they're looking for safety. Seven, do I understand enough about what he's going to do to be comfortable? I don't really need to know how you move the money through the purple pipe and the pink pipe, but I kind of got to at least know there's pipes. Am I making the best comparative choice, which obviously you don't want anybody going comparison shopping, so you need to answer this question for them in your copy one way or another. And last, am I paying a fair price? Very few people actually care about, am I paying the cheapest price? The only people who really care about that? It's the governing item for about 12 to 14% depending on the economy of the population. It is the people who have no alternative whatsoever. They have to get the cheapest price because they have to get the cheapest price because they are poor. And probably none of us want them as customers anyway. If you want poor people as customers, that's social work. Get some sandals, get a robe, head out, but we don't want poor people as customers. Now, some of them will sneak in and that's okay because sometimes we can't decide. There's people in this room who entered this room for the first time poor and are no longer poor. But if we have a choice, better to start with somebody who can afford to buy something that isn't necessarily the cheapest price. So that's what people want to know. So to wrap this up, copy that will persuade people to buy intangibles that have relatively no intrinsic value. It's not a vegematic. It's not a thing we hang on the wall and admire. It's not a piece of furniture we sit in. It's not the business we're in. The copy that sells that has to get into people's psyche and emotions and has to manipulate the things that already are in there to an enormous degree. Now, one last thing for you. Loops all the way back around to our ethical considerations. So here's one of the difficult things about this business. We know in advance that many, probably the majority, will not get the promised outcomes. If we play fair, we are delivering something to them they could get the promised outcomes from, but we know in advance most won't. One of my earliest businesses in this industry, we actually produced audio cassette programs we were a manufacturer for all sorts of speakers and companies. The Unity Church, by the way, Nelson, was one of our biggest clients. If I haven't told you, I have a very funny Unity Church cassette story that we will share at another time. So we used to joke all the time that in reality, if you're manufacturing an 8 cassette program for somebody, we really only needed to duplicate the first tape and then put 7 blanks in. If you ship 10,000 of those albums, you would hear from roughly 8, 10, 12 people who would complain that they had blank tapes. You then apologize for the manufacturing defect and send them out and everybody else no harm, no foul because they never listened to them anyway. Now we never actually did that except once or twice by accident, which is how I know what the actual percentage is of people calling saying we got blank tapes. We were a pretty dysfunctional company. So we know in advance that the majority are not going to get the promised outcomes. To succeed, to push as hard as you can push, to sell as effectively as you can sell, this can't bother you. It is irrelevant. It is not your responsibility or my responsibility. You didn't have the funny given birth to them. They ain't going to come visit you in the old folks home. You do not have that level of parental responsibility for your customers. You only have the responsibility to play fair if they follow directions. Now here's the trouble. We can't predict which one will. Often it's surprising. Therefore we can't prejudge them. We can't soft sell some who appear weak and wounded to us and hard sell people who have money falling out of their pocket. And so it won't hurt anybody to take some of it. No, no, no, no, no. We got to get everyone we could decide is in our target market. We got to do everything humanly possible to get them and then let them sort themselves out as they will. So the end piece of advice I have for you about copy is don't pull a punch. Do not pull a punch. Don't let yourself feel, no, use everything you got to make the sale and then let them sort themselves out. I believe you have a break. I believe we're two minutes over so share your journal and get back in here in a hurry. And thank you very much.
